# Kage / 影

> Kage is a free web translator that encodes any language into the 忍びいろは (Shinobi Iroha) — the 49-symbol kanji cipher recorded in the 1676 Japanese ninja encyclopedia Bansenshukai (萬川集海) — and decodes the cipher back.

Kage translates user input into Japanese, reduces it to hiragana, and substitutes each kana for its corresponding Shinobi Iroha glyph. The decoder reverses the substitution and translates the resulting Japanese back into the chosen output language. The UI is available in English, French, Spanish, German, and Japanese.

## Pages

- [Kage translator (home)](/): Encode any language into the Shinobi Iroha ninja cipher, or decode the cipher back to your language.
- [Bansenshukai history](/bansenshukai-history): A short history of the 1676 ninja encyclopedia Bansenshukai and the 49-symbol Shinobi Iroha cipher.
- [Ninja symbols reference](/ninja-symbols): Visual reference of the 49 Shinobi Iroha symbols (ninja alphabet) with their hiragana, romaji, and meaning.
